document.title: Molecular cloning and sequence analysis of Factor C cDNA from the Singapore horseshoe crab, Carcinoscorpius rotundicauda

authors.name: Jeak L. Ding
authors.affiliation: Marine Biotechnology Laboratory / BioScience Centre, Department of Zoology, National University of Singapore

authors.name: M. Anthony A. Navas III
authors.affiliation: Department of Zoology, National University of Singapore (present address: Dept of Biochemistry, Baylor College, Houston, TX)

authors.name: Bow Ho
authors.affiliation: Department of Microbiology, National University of Singapore

institutions.name: National University of Singapore
institutions.note: Depts of Zoology & Microbiology; Marine Biotechnology Lab

institutions.name: Baylor College
institutions.note: present address of Navas; low priority

methods.name: Amoebocyte procurement & total/poly(A)+ RNA preparation
methods.params: cardiac bleed into 3% NaCl + N-ethylmaleimide; guanidinium isothiocyanate lysis; CsCl ultracentrifugation; Oligo-dT selection; pyrogen-free 0-4C

methods.name: Directional cDNA synthesis and cloning into lambda-gt22
methods.params: Gubler & Hoffman; NotI primer-adaptors; EcoRI adaptors; directional ligation into lambda-gt22; E. coli LE392/Y1090

methods.name: Library screening / plaque hybridization (heterologous TtFC53 probe)
methods.params: 32P TtFC53 probe; 40 presumptive clones, 16 studied; Factor C = 0.03% of library

methods.name: Restriction mapping of CrFC clones
methods.params: EcoRI/NotI orientation maps; full maps CrFC21/26 vs TtFC53/41

methods.name: Subcloning EE and EN fragments into pGEM 11Zf(+)
methods.params: E. coli JM109; orientation via XhoI; pEE/pEN

methods.name: DNA sequencing (Sanger dideoxy, both strands) with exonuclease deletions
methods.params: Sanger 1977; Exo/ExoEN deletion subclones; T7/SP6 primers

methods.name: Northern blot analysis of amoebocyte RNA
methods.params: glyoxal denaturation; heterologous TtFC53 probe (low stringency 42C) vs homologous CrFC26 EE / 368-bp probe (high stringency 65C)

methods.name: Southern blot hybridization of CrFC clones
methods.params: EcoRI+NotI digests; TtFC53 then 3'-EN / TtFC41 reprobe

methods.name: EMBL gene-bank homology search of catalytic domain
Computational mRNA secondary-structure prediction of 5' UTR
methods.params: folding of CrFC21/26 5' ends; AUG accessibility

methods.name: Kyte-Doolittle hydropathy analysis of N-terminus
methods.params: first 59 residues of CrFC26; window=7; signal-peptide ID

methods.name: In vitro transcription-coupled-translation (TnT) expression assay
methods.params: T7-driven CrFC21 vs CrFC26; 'unpublished data, patent pending'

methods.name: Domain / catalytic-triad structural annotation by homology
methods.params: domain boundaries (Furie & Furie); triad by trypsin analogy; specificity from Asp at trypsin-189-equivalent

preparations.name: C. rotundicauda Factor C cDNA, long form
preparations.note: 4182 bp; 568-nt 5'UTR (7 false ATGs); ORF 3249 nt; signal 24aa + zymogen 1059 res; calc MW 120,244 Da

preparations.name: C. rotundicauda Factor C cDNA, short form
preparations.note: 3448 bp; lacks most 5'UTR; exposed AUG/Kozak; 5' identical to TtFC53; distinct mRNA, not a truncation

preparations.name: T. tridentatus Factor C cDNA, 5' partial (Muta 1991)
preparations.note: external reference clone, 2470 bp; heterologous probe; cross-species comparison spine

preparations.name: T. tridentatus Factor C cDNA, 3' partial (Muta 1991)
C. rotundicauda clone CrFC45 (non-cross-hybridizing)
preparations.note: homologous only to 5' EE region; no internal EcoRI

preparations.name: C. rotundicauda clone CrFC63 (non-cross-hybridizing)
preparations.note: not similar to other CrFC in 3' region

preparations.name: C. rotundicauda amoebocyte total/poly(A)+ RNA
preparations.note: Northern ~4.0 kNt mRNA (+15.8, 5.9 kNt low-stringency bands)

claims.text: Factor C cDNA is a rare-copy gene, only 0.03% of the C. rotundicauda amoebocyte library.
claims.quote: only 0.03% of the cDNA library was positive for Factor C cDNA, indicating its status as a rare copy gene

claims.text: Two distinct Factor C cDNA forms were cloned: CrFC26 (4182 bp) and CrFC21 (3448 bp).
claims.quote: Two forms of Factor C cDNAs: CrFC21 (3448 bp) and CrFC26 (4182 bp) have been cloned into lambda gt22.

claims.text: CrFC26 = 568 nt 5'UTR (7 false-start ATGs), 3249-nt ORF, stop, 365 nt 3'UTR.
claims.quote: 568 nucleotides of 5' untranslated sequence containing seven ATGs before the real initiation site, an ORF of 3249 nucleotides ... and 365 nucleotides of 3' untranslated sequence

claims.text: CrFC26 ORF encodes a 24-aa signal peptide + 1059-residue zymogen (1083 aa total).
claims.quote: The ORF codes for a signal peptide of 24 amino acids and a Factor C zymogen of 1059 residues

claims.text: Unglycosylated CrFC26 zymogen calc MW 120,244 Da, close to purified native enzyme MW.
claims.quote: prior to N-glycosylation, has a calculated molecular weight of 120,244 daltons. This is close to the estimated molecular weight of single- and double-chain Factor C enzymes purified from the amoebocytes

claims.text: CrFC contains six potential N-glycosylation sites (Asn-Xaa-Ser/Thr), as in TtFC.
claims.quote: similar to TtFC, there are six potential N-glycosylation sites within the CrFC sequence Asn-Xaa-Ser/Thr

claims.text: CrFC has a serine-protease catalytic triad Asp-His-Ser (His873-Asp929-Ser1030 in CrFC26).
claims.quote: also contain the catalytic triad, His809-Asp865-Ser966 for CrFC21 and His873-Asp929-Ser1030 for CrFC26

claims.text: The endotoxin-activation cleavage site is between Phe-Ile (F826-I827 in CrFC26).
claims.quote: The unique proteolytic site due to endotoxin-activation of the Factor C enzyme is found between Phe-Ile: F762-I763 for CrFC21, and F826-I827 for CrFC26

claims.text: CrFC and TtFC Factor C cDNAs share 97.7% overall sequence homology.
claims.quote: an overall homology of 97.7%

claims.text: The 3'UTR is the most divergent region (only 86% homology).
claims.quote: Most of the dissimilarities ... were found in the 3' UTR, where a lower percentage homology of 86% was observed.

claims.text: CrFC26 has an extra 716 nt at the 5' end (and 64 extra aa upstream of start Met) vs TtFC53.
claims.quote: the C. rotundicauda cDNA was found to be longer by 716 nucleotides at the 5' end, and it also has an extra 64 amino acids upstream from the starting met

claims.text: CrFC21 and TtFC53 share identical 5'-end sequence -> CrFC21 is a distinct mRNA, not a truncated CrFC26.
claims.quote: CrFC21 was not merely a truncated species of CrFC26; rather, it was derived from a totally distinct species of mRNA that is exactly identical to the one that gave rise to the T. tridentatus Factor C cDNA

claims.text: Northern (low stringency, TtFC53) shows three bands (15.8, 5.9, 4.0 kNt); high-stringency homologous probe shows only 4.0 kNt.
claims.quote: TtFC probe under low stringency of 42C showed three bands corresponding to 15.8, 5.9, and 4.0 kNt ... hybridization under high stringency with homologous EE fragment of CrFC26 revealed only the 4.0 kNt RNA band

claims.text: A 368-bp 5'-end probe hybridizes to the ~4 kNt mRNA, confirming the cDNA is full-length.
claims.quote: A single band of approximately 4 kNt ... indicates that the cDNA isolated is full-length and the entire 5' end of CrFC26 unequivocally belongs to this species of Factor C

claims.text: CrFC26 has four polyadenylation signals (AATAAA at nt 183, 239, 2474, 4142; functional one 19 nt upstream of poly(A)).
claims.quote: the canonical hexanucleotide sequence AATAAA is present 19 nucleotides upstream from the polyadenylation site (at nucleotide position 4142). There are three other AATAAA sequences found at nucleotide positions 183, 239, and 2474

claims.text: Restriction maps differ by species: CrFC has 2 BamHI sites vs 1 in TtFC53; CrFC26 has 2 HindIII vs 3 in TtFC53.
claims.quote: CrFC has two BamHI sites compared with only one in TtFC53 ... only has two HindIII sites compared with three in the latter

claims.text: Single base substitutions explain inter-species restriction-site differences (BamHI 1549, HindIII 1724/2443, etc.).
claims.quote: the absence of the BamHI (GGATCC) site in TtFC ... was due to the substitution of the first G with a T in TtFC ... a C-to-T base substitution ... responsible for the loss of the third HindIII

claims.text: Two clones (CrFC45, CrFC63) lack an internal EcoRI site and do not cross-hybridize with the 3'/TtFC41 probe.
claims.quote: CrFC45 and CrFC63, did not cross-hybridize with either probe ... homologous to only the 5'-end region (EE fragment)

claims.text: CrFC26 5'UTR folds into hairpins obscuring the start codon; CrFC21 presents an exposed AUG.
claims.quote: Numerous hairpin stems and loops are seen for the CrFC26, obscuring its real start codon, whereas CrFC21 showed a well-exposed AUG start site

claims.text: Kyte-Doolittle hydropathy of residues 1-59 shows a hydrophobic peak over 1-24, the putative signal peptide.
claims.quote: the first 24 residues to be the putative signal peptide rich in hydrophobic amino acids

claims.text: CrFC26 has a mosaic architecture: 5 short consensus repeats, 1 lectin domain, 1 EGF-like domain, Cys-rich + Pro-rich regions, serine-protease domain (827-1083); no propeptide/kringle/finger.
claims.quote: five short consensus repeats ... one lectin-domain (500-632), one EGF-like domain (167-200) ... The serine protease domain is found at positions 827-1083. No propeptide sequence, kringle domains ... or finger domains were found

claims.text: EMBL search: CrFC catalytic domain closest to TtFC (97.7%/777 bp), then thrombins/prothrombins (~52-57%); human hepsin 59.0%/278 bp.
claims.quote: structurally closest to T. tridentatus Factor C, with 97.7% homology in 777 overlapping nucleotides (Table 1)

claims.text: CrFC is structurally closest to prothrombin/thrombin but catalytically trypsin-like, attributed to Asp1024 (analogous to trypsin Asp189).
claims.quote: the CrFC is structurally closest to prothrombin and thrombin ... explained by the Asp1024 ... analogous to Asp189 in trypsin ... substrate specificity similar to that of trypsin

claims.text: High Factor C DNA homology substantiates close molecular-evolutionary proximity of C. rotundicauda and T. tridentatus despite morphological/ecological/taxonomic disparity.
claims.quote: The high degree of homology between Factor C from T. tridentatus and C. rotundicauda substantiates, at the molecular level, the proximity of these two species ... This finding contravenes the apparent disparities with respect to their morphology, ecological habitat, and taxonomical classification.

claims.text: The two CrFC cDNA forms arise from differential splicing of the primary transcript around its 5' terminus.
claims.quote: the existence of the two types of C. rotundicauda Factor C cDNA could be attributable to differential splicing of the initial primary transcript around its 5' terminal

claims.text: Recombinant C. rotundicauda Factor C is commercially significant as the basis of a rapid endotoxin-detection kit for pharmaceutical QA.
claims.quote: recombinant Factor C from C. rotundicauda would be of great significance to the pharmaceutical industry, in which pyrogen-detection constitutes an inherent part of quality assurance

claims.text: CrFC21 constructs express more Factor C than CrFC26 in vitro (absence of false-start ATGs, accessible AUG, Kozak consensus).
claims.quote: higher expression of Factor C from CrFC21 constructs (our unpublished data, patent pending) ... due to the absence of multiple false-start ATGs ... the more easily accessible AUG, and the presence of a consensus Kozak sequence

claims.text: C. rotundicauda amoebocyte lysate is reportedly more sensitive to endotoxin than Limulus or Tachypleus lysates (Kim et al. 1988).
claims.quote: its amoebocyte lysate has been found to be more sensitive to endotoxin than the Limulus or Tachypleus amoebocyte lysates (Kim et al., 1988)

cites.ref: Muta et al. 1991, Limulus (Tachypleus) Factor C mosaic structure, J Biol Chem 266:6554
Nakamura, Morita, Iwanaga 1986, LPS-sensitive Factor C zymogen, Eur J Biochem 154:511
Nakamura et al. 1988, Factor C activation by lipid A; endotoxin-binding heavy chain
Ding, Navas, Ho 1993a, two forms of Factor C purification/characterisation, BBA 1202:149
Kim, Ding, Ho 1988, endotoxin activation of C. rotundicauda clotting proteins
Srimal et al. 1985, coagulogen aa sequence of C. rotundicauda
Furie & Furie 1988, molecular basis of blood coagulation

resolution_candidates.extracted: Factor C (C. rotundicauda) / recombinant Factor C
resolution_candidates.action: flag - foundational CrFC cloning; relate to :rFC, do NOT merge

resolution_candidates.extracted: CITATION TIE: Mizumura 2017 (PMC5302069) cites Ding & Ho rFC work

conflict_of_interest.structural_coi: academic (NUS) authors; NOT employed by a reagent manufacturer; BUT disclose a 'patent pending' on the favoured CrFC21 expression construct (NC4)
conflict_of_interest.action: low overall COI; mildly down-weight the CrFC21>CrFC26 expression claim (NC4) given the pending patent

provenance.recommended_next: verify-extraction; then load - NOTE loader must key on pmid (doi is null)
